title: What it costs
intro: In Canadian dollars, reference rate £1 ≈ CAD 1.85 (July 2026 — re-check before booking). Costed for one traveller, solo, at the comfort level this trip is built for: clean, warm, well-run places rather than luxury, seafood dinners without flinching, and two or three deliberate splurges because it's a fiftieth. Twenty-one nights, 20 May – 10 June 2027.

statCards.label: Book the car deck in January

lines.label: Flights (open-jaw)
lines.category: Into Edinburgh (EDI), home from Inverness (INV) — no backtracking. Via a European or direct UK hub; book 5–6 months out for late May.

lines.label: Accommodation — 21 nights
lines.category: Edinburgh 5n (~CAD 175–435, one night aboard Fingal at ~CAD 700) · Argyll 5n (~CAD 165–315, one at Portavadie ~CAD 400) · Skye transit 1n (~CAD 220) · Harris 5n (Rock House splurge ~CAD 425) · Assynt 5n (~CAD 160–400). Solo occupancy, single supplement included.

lines.label: Car hire — 16 days
lines.category: Picked up at Edinburgh airport on day 6, returned at Inverness airport. Compact — the Drumbeg and Hushinish single-tracks reward a small car. One-way drop fee included. Full insurance.

lines.label: Fuel
lines.category: ~1 800 km of west coast and islands. Petrol ~£1.60/litre (~CAD 2.95), and it costs more on Harris and in Assynt than on the mainland — the pumps are scarce, not cheap.

lines.label: Ferries (CalMac)
lines.category: Uig → Tarbert with the car (~CAD 90) · Stornoway → Ullapool with the car (~CAD 145) · plus the small Portavadie–Tarbert and Bute hops (~CAD 60). Vehicle space MUST be booked the day 2027 sales open — roughly the third Friday of January.

lines.label: Food and drink
lines.category: Coffee and a bakery morning, a proper lunch (a hatch, a shed, a harbour wall), seafood dinner most nights: ~CAD 120/day average, with room for Scarista House and the Kilberry Inn (~CAD 100–130 each) and the Dulse seafood boil.

lines.label: Activities and tastings
lines.category: Port of Leith and Harris distillery tours · the Portobello sauna · a Summer Isles boat (~CAD 85) or the full St Kilda sail (~CAD 575, weather permitting) · Inveraray · Kilmartin.

lines.label: Travel insurance
lines.category: Solo, full medical coverage, 22 days, over-50 rate.

scenarios.name: Trimmed
scenarios.desc: ~10 200 $ — skip Fingal and Portavadie, take the guesthouse tier throughout, cook two or three nights on Harris (the Rock House has a kitchen), and choose the short Summer Isles boat over St Kilda.

scenarios.name: As built
scenarios.desc: ~12 450 $ — the itinerary as written: three deliberate splurges (a night aboard the Fingal, a night at Portavadie, the Rock House on Harris), seafood dinners without doing the arithmetic, and both distillery tours.

scenarios.name: No arithmetic at all
scenarios.desc: ~14 500 $ — Scarista House with dinner, the full St Kilda day, the Kilberry Inn with the wine pairing, an extra night on Harris, and a car you don't wince at on the single-tracks.

advice: Two line items are non-negotiable and time-sensitive: the CalMac car deck (Uig–Tarbert sells out months ahead — book the day 2027 sales open, around the third Friday of January) and the Harris accommodation (the island has very few beds; the good ones go 6–12 months out). Everything else can wait. The honest way to trim this budget is not to eat worse — it's to sleep more simply, since Scotland's guesthouses at CAD 165 are frequently warmer and better run than its hotels at CAD 400.
